# contractwatch

> Federal contract award data sourced from USASpending.gov.

contractwatch aggregates publicly reported federal contract award records and surfaces pattern-based observations against the underlying data. All source data is public and available directly from USASpending.gov.

## About

contractwatch screens every federal contract award above $1M against three patterns: F01 (no prior federal history plus sole-source above $10M), F02 (no prior federal history plus competitive solicitation with one offer above $25M), F03 (no prior federal history plus first contract above $25M). A structural filter removes recipients that fire these patterns by design. Federal-prime subsidiaries, joint ventures, municipal authorities, healthcare providers, ANC and tribal entities, and foreign government recipients are excluded so the dashboard surfaces cases that lack a structural explanation. Flagging is descriptive, not accusatory.

## Updates

The site attempts to refresh daily. A scheduled scan reads new awards from USASpending and re-runs the pattern checks. Coverage goes back to October 2017 (start of FY18). The "Last scan" date on the dashboard reflects the most recent award action date present in the dataset.

## Data sources

- [USASpending.gov API](https://api.usaspending.gov/) - prime contract awards
- [USASpending.gov bulk archives](https://files.usaspending.gov/award_data_archive/) - per-fiscal-year contract action snapshots used for the initial database build

## Endpoints

- [/](https://contractwatch.org/) - main data view
- [/data/latest.json](https://contractwatch.org/data/latest.json) - current flagged-award snapshot as JSON
- [/data/stats.json](https://contractwatch.org/data/stats.json) - aggregate statistics across the snapshot
- [/sitemap.xml](https://contractwatch.org/sitemap.xml)
- [/robots.txt](https://contractwatch.org/robots.txt)

## Source code

- [github.com/contractwatch](https://github.com/contractwatch) - repositories

## Verification

Every row links back to its corresponding USASpending.gov award record. Readers are encouraged to consult the upstream record directly.

## FAQ

Q: How are awards selected for scanning?
A: Every federal contract award with obligation above $1M is read from USASpending. The three pattern checks then run against each one. Awards that match a pattern and survive the structural filter are flagged.

Q: Does a flag mean wrongdoing?
A: No. A flag means the award matches a pattern that, statistically, is uncommon at the dollar threshold for the flag. Many flagged awards have routine explanations. The flagging is a starting point for inquiry, not a finding.

Q: How often does the data update?
A: A scheduled scan attempts to refresh the dataset daily. If a scan fails (upstream outage, API key rotation, deploy error) the previous data remains visible until the next successful run.

Q: Why are some obvious "no prior history" entities not flagged?
A: They match a structural-filter pattern (municipal authority, hospital, joint venture, federal-prime subsidiary, ANC/tribal entity, foreign government recipient) where the absence of prior federal history is explained by the entity type rather than by anomaly.
